frameworks.name: memory reinstatement
frameworks.one_line: Confidence relies on reactivating traces of stored memories

frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: Shared neural representations between novel and learned items index confidence

frameworks.name: semantic gist account of false memory
frameworks.one_line: False memories arise from high-level semantic overlap with stored items

frameworks.name: perceptual gist account
frameworks.one_line: Posterior visual regions encode perceptual rather than confidence-driving gist

core_question: What neural basis supports confidence in false memories given that they were never stored?
key_assumptions: Memory confidence requires a neural trace of stored content
Shared neural representations index reinstatement of learned memories
False memory confidence dissociates from veridical storage
manipulations: Presented learned vs novel objects during recognition
Compared trials by recognition response (learned vs non-learned)
Modulated confidence levels reported by participants
Contrasted prefrontal vs posterior visual regions
key_findings: For novel objects, false memory confidence scaled with shared prefrontal representations with learned objects
Posterior visual cortex did not show this confidence-dependent shared representation
Confidence-dependent shared representations were absent when novel objects were correctly identified as non-learned
interpretations: False memory confidence is based on prefrontal reinstatement of high-level semantic gist of stored memories
Perceptual gist in posterior regions is dissociable from confidence-driving semantic reinstatement
